    <!-- Snippet: legacy-headEnd -->
<meta name="color-scheme" content="dark">
<style data-override-sandpack>
/* For iOS: prevent browser zoom when clicking on sandbox.
   Does NOT apply to code blocks. */
@supports (-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch) {
  @media screen and (max-width: 768px) {
    .cm-content {
      font-size: inherit !important;
    }
  }
}
</style>
<style>
body {
  --black: #0f1115;
  --white: #f5f5f5;
  --yellow: #fff312;
  --hue-0: var(--yellow);
  --hue-1: #ff0088;
  --hue-2: #dd00ee;
  --hue-3: #9911ff;
  --hue-4: #0d63f8;
  --hue-5: #0cdcf7;
  --hue-6: #4ff0b7;

  --hue-0-transparent: #fff31244;
  --hue-1-transparent: #ff008844;
  --hue-2-transparent: #dd00ee44;
  --hue-3-transparent: #9911ff44;
  --hue-4-transparent: #0d63f844;
  --hue-5-transparent: #0cdcf744;
  --hue-6-transparent: #4ff0b744;

    --accent: var(--hue-0);
    --accent-transparent: var(--hue-0-transparent);
}

html, .cm-scroller {
    scrollbar-color: var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, rgb(235, 235, 235)) transparent;
}

.cm-scroller {
    scrollbar-width: thin;
}

blockquote p {
  z-index: 1;
  position: relative;
}

blockquote .framer-text {
  margin-top: 20px !important;
}

blockquote::after {
  content: "â";
  color: var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
  border-radius: 50%;
  width: 56px;
  height: 200px;
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  font-size: 300px;
  line-height: 200px;
}

.spring-visualiser {
  padding: 20px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 10px;
  background-color: var(--token-74b02f5b-961b-49eb-8e13-4477bd831f3e, #12141a);
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container {
  position: relative;
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container::before  {
  --size: 5px;
  position: absolute;
  content: "";
  right: 18px;
  pointer-events: none;
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container::before {
  border-left: var(--size) solid transparent;
  border-right: var(--size) solid transparent;
  border-top: var(--size) solid var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  top: 18px;
}

.spring-visualiser label {
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  font-family: Gabarito;
  font-size: 16px;
  gap: 5px;
  margin: 10px 0;
}

.spring-visualiser input, .input {
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  font-family: "Azeret Mono", monospace;
  font-size: 14px;
}

.spring-visualiser select, .select {
  appearance: none;
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  width: 100%;
  font-family: Gabarito;
  font-size: 16px;
  padding: 10px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 5px;
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=number]:focus-visible,
.spring-visualiser select:focus-visible {
  outline: 1px solid rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=number]:focus-visible,
.select:focus-visible {
  outline: 1px solid var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.spring-visualiser .inputs {
  display: flex;
  gap: 5px;
  padding-top: 5px;
}

.spring-visualiser .inputs input[type=number], .input[type=number] {
  appearance: none;
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  font-family: "Azeret Mono", monospace;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 5px;
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  padding: 7px 9px;
  width: 80px;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range], .input[type=range] {
  -webkit-appearance: none; 
  width: 200px;
  background: transparent;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
}

.spring-visualiser > div:nth-child(2) {
  padding: 40px 0 0 0!important;
}

/* Webkit */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  background: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  border-radius: 50%;
  cursor: grab;
  margin-top: -5.5px;
}

/* All the same stuff for Firefox */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-moz-range-thumb, .input[type=range]::-moz-range-thumb {
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  background: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  border-radius: 50%;
  cursor: grab;
}

/* Track styles */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-runnable-track, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
  width: 100%;
  height: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-radius: 2px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]:focus::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
 background-color: rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=range]:focus::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
  background-color: var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-moz-range-track, .input[type=range]::-moz-range-track {
  width: 100%;
  height: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-radius: 2px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
}

.spring-visualiser label.use-visual-duration {
  flex-direction: row;
  display: flex;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=checkbox] {
  accent-color: rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=checkbox] {
  accent-color: var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.__framer-search-modal-container > div:first-child {
  background-color: transparent !important;
  background-image: radial-gradient(transparent 1px, #000 1px) !important;
  background-size: 4px 4px !important;
  backdrop-filter: brightness(1) blur(10px);
}

::selection {
  color: var(--white);
  background: var(--hue-4);
}
</style>

<!-- SnippetEnd: legacy-headEnd -->

    <!-- End of headEnd -->
</head>

dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">These work identically to their HTML/SVG counterparts - same props, same behaviour - but with additional animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The most common animation prop is <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>. When values passed to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> change, the element will automatically animate to that value.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" framer-DuX84 framer-yYwXt framer-6lz8w framer-9rL7Y framer-K9nYC framer-mVE8v framer-pt9tr framer-N8luP framer-pvjhpf framer-v-pvjhpf" data-border="true" data-framer-name="Note" style="--border-bottom-width:1px;--border-color:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));--border-left-width:1px;--border-right-width:1px;--border-style:solid;--border-top-width:1px;background-color:rgba(103, 219, 240, 0.2);border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px"><div class="framer-gv0uwh"><svg class="framer-QVbbS framer-bpvkia" role="presentation" style="--1m973uw:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));--js9iwy:2" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#746265957"/></svg></div><div class="framer-vb9j5w"><div class="framer-pwf8a9" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1l6bcxe">For SVG components, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">y</code> <strong class="framer-text">attributes</strong> can be set using <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrX</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrY</code>. <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./react-svg-animation">Learn more about SVG animations in React</a><!--/$-->.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="supported-value-types" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#supported-value-types" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Supported value types</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion can animate any of the following value types:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">100</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Strings containing numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0vh"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"10px"</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors: All CSS color formats like hex, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rgba</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">hsla</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklch</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklab</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color-mix</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex strings containing multiple numbers and/or colors (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">box-shadow</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">display: "none"/"block"</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">visibility: "hidden"/"visible"</code>.</p></li></ul><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="value-type-conversion" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#value-type-conversion" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Value type conversion</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In general, values can only be animated between two of the same type (i.e <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code> to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"100px"</code>).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors can be freely animated between hex, RGBA and HSLA types.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Additionally, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code>, <code <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code> can animate between different value types.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="transitions" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#transitions" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Transitions</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, Motion will create appropriate transitions for snappy animations based on the type of value being animated.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For instance, physical properties like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code> are animated with spring physics, whereas values like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color</code> are animated with duration-based easing curves.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">However, you can define your own animations via <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">transition</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions"> prop</a><!--/$-->.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="exit-animations" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#exit-animations" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Exit animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React can animate elements as they're removed from the DOM.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In React, when a component is removed, it's usually removed instantly. Motion provides <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">AnimatePresence</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence"> component</a><!--/$--> which keeps elements in the DOM while they perform an animation defined with the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> prop.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/exit-animation?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Keyframes</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">So far, we've set animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> to single values, like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity: 0</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This is great when we want to animate from the current value to a new value. But sometimes we want to animate through a <strong class="framer-text">series of values</strong>. In animation terms, these are called <strong class="framer-text">keyframes</strong>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">All animation props can accept keyframe arrays:</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, initial value as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>. src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/keyframes-wildcards?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, if a keyframe animation is interrupting another animation, the transition will feel more natural.</p><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="wildcard-keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#wildcard-keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Wildcard keyframes</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">null</code> keyframe is called a <strong class="framer-text">wildcard keyframe</strong>. A wildcard keyframe simply takes the value before it (or the current value, if this is the first keyframe in the array).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Wildcard keyframes can be useful for holding a value mid-animation without having to repeat values.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div timing</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, each keyframe is spaced evenly throughout the animation. You can override this by setting <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions#times">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions#times">times</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions#times"> option</a><!--/$--> via <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transition</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">times</code> is an array of progress values between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> is the start of the animation, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> is the end of the animation. class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Gesture animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React has animation props that can define how an element animates when it <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-gestures">recognises a gesture</a><!--/$-->.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Supported gestures are:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileTap</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileFocus</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileDrag</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileInView</code></p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">When a gesture starts, it animates to the values defined in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">while-</code>, and then when the gesture ends it animates back to the values in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">initial</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> prop works well for single elements, but real interfaces often need coordinated animations across parent and child components. Variants solve this by defining named animation states that propagate through the component tree.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto;aspect-ratio:560 / 315;--aspect-ratio:560 / 315" data-width="fill"><article style="position:relative;width:100%;height:100%;border-radius:0px;transform:unset;cursor:pointer;overflow:hidden"><div style="position:absolute;top:0;left:0;height:100%;width:100%;background:center / cover url(https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/U4AIQ92NFkI/maxresdefault.webp) no-repeat"></div><button aria-label="Play" style="position:absolute;top:50%;left:50%;transform:translate(-50%, -50%);width:68px;height:48px;padding:0;border:none;background:transparent;cursor:pointer"><svg height="100%" version="1.1" viewBox="0 0 68 48" width="100%"><path d="M66.52,7.74c-0.78-2.93-2.49-5.41-5.42-6.19C55.79,.13,34,0,34,0S12.21,.13,6.9,1.55 C3.97,2.33,2.27,4.81,1.48,7.74C0.06,13.05,0,24,0,24s0.06,10.95,1.48,16.26c0.78,2.93,2.49,5.41,5.42,6.19 C12.21,47.87,34,48,34,48s21.79-0.13,27.1-1.55c2.93-0.78,4.64-3.26,5.42-6.19C67.94,34.95,68,24,68,24S67.94,13.05,66.52,7.74z" fill="#212121" fill-opacity="0.8" style="transition:fill .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1), fill-opacity .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1)"/><path d="M 45,24 27,14 27,34" fill="#fff"/></svg></button></article></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants are a set of named targets. style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#propagation" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Propagation</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants are useful for reusing and combining animation targets. But it becomes powerful for orchestrating animations throughout trees.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants will flow down through <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components. So in this example when the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ul</code> enters the viewport, all of its children with a "visible" variant will also animate in:</p><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, variants can be resolved differently for each animating element.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="animation-controls" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#animation-controls" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Animation controls</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Declarative animations via <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code> cover most UI interactions. For cases that need sequencing, timeline scrubbing, or triggering animations from events outside React's render cycle, the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate">useAnimate</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate"> hook</a><!--/$-->  provides imperative controls:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Animating any HTML/SVG element (not just <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex animation sequences.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Controlling animations with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">time</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">speed</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">play()</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pause()</code> and other playback controls.</p></li></ul><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This avoids React re-renders entirely. The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component updates the DOM text node directly, making it suitable for high-frequency value changes like counters or live data.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/number-radix-slider?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="next" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#next" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Next</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In this guide we've covered the basic kinds of animations we can perform in Motion using its <strong class="framer-text">animation props</strong>. However, there's much more to discover.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Most of the examples on this page have used HTML elements, but Motion also has unique <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-svg-animation">SVG animation</a><!--/$--> features, like its simple line drawing API.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">We've also only covered time-based animations, but Motion also provides powerful <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-scroll-animations">scroll animation</a><!--/$--> features like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">useScroll</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileInView</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">It also provides a powerful <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-layout-animations">layout animation</a><!--/$--> engine, that can animate between any two layouts using performant transforms.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Finally, there's also a whole <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="https://motion.dev/examples?platform=react#fundamentals" rel="noopener">Fundamentals examples category</a><!--/$--> that covers all the basics of animating with Motion for React with live demos and copy-paste code.</p></div></div><div class="ssr-variant hidden-uq7ul hidden-1i9x81p"><div class="framer-a4hymo" data-framer-name="Content" style="transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="../">Motion for React</a><!--/$--> is a simple yet powerful animation library. Whether you're building hover effects, scroll-triggered animations, or complex animation sequences, this guide will provide an overview of all the ways you can animate in React with Motion.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="what-you-ll-learn" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#what-you-ll-learn" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">What you'll learn</a></h2><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to create your first animation with the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">&lt;motion.div /&gt;</code> component.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Which values and elements you can animate.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to customise your animations with transition options.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to animate elements as they enter and exit the DOM.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to orchestrate animations with variants.</p></li></ul><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">These work identically to their HTML/SVG counterparts - same props, same behaviour - but with additional animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The most common animation prop is <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>. When values passed to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> change, the element will automatically animate to that value.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="--border-bottom-width:1px;--border-color:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));--border-left-width:1px;--border-right-width:1px;--border-style:solid;--border-top-width:1px;background-color:rgba(103, 219, 240, 0.2);border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px"><div class="framer-gv0uwh"><svg class="framer-QVbbS framer-bpvkia" role="presentation" style="--1m973uw:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));--js9iwy:2" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#746265957"/></svg></div><div class="framer-vb9j5w"><div class="framer-pwf8a9" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1l6bcxe">For SVG components, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">y</code> <strong class="framer-text">attributes</strong> can be set using <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrX</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrY</code>. <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./react-svg-animation">Learn more about SVG animations in React</a><!--/$-->.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="supported-value-types" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#supported-value-types" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Supported value types</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion can animate any of the following value types:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">100</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Strings containing numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0vh"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"10px"</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors: All CSS color formats like hex, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rgba</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">hsla</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklch</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklab</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color-mix</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex strings containing multiple numbers and/or colors (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">box-shadow</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">display: "none"/"block"</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">visibility: "hidden"/"visible"</code>.</p></li></ul><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="value-type-conversion" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#value-type-conversion" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Value type conversion</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In general, values can only be animated between two of the same type (i.e <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code> to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"100px"</code>).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors can be freely animated between hex, RGBA and HSLA types.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Additionally, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">width</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">height</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">top</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code> can animate between different value types.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">If animating <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">height: auto</code> while also animating <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">display</code> in to/out of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">"none"</code>, replace this with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">visibility</code> <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">"hidden"</code> as elements with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">display: none</code> can't be measured.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="transform-origin" framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transform-origin</code> has three shortcut values that can be set and animated individually:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="transitions" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#transitions" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Transitions</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, Motion will create appropriate transitions for snappy animations based on the type of value being animated.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For instance, physical properties like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code> are animated with spring physics, whereas values like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color</code> are animated with duration-based easing curves.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">However, you can define your own animations via <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">transition</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions"> prop</a><!--/$-->.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="exit-animations" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#exit-animations" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Exit animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React can animate elements as they're removed from the DOM.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In React, when a component is removed, it's usually removed instantly. Motion provides <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">AnimatePresence</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence"> component</a><!--/$--> which keeps elements in the DOM while they perform an animation defined with the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> prop.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/exit-animation?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Keyframes</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">So far, we've set animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> to single values, like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity: 0</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This is great when we want to animate from the current value to a new value. But sometimes we want to animate through a <strong class="framer-text">series of values</strong>. In animation terms, these are called <strong class="framer-text">keyframes</strong>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">All animation props can accept keyframe arrays:</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, array of values, the element will animate through each of these values in sequence.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In the previous example, we explicitly set the initial value as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>. But we can also say "use the current value" by setting the first value to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">null</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/keyframes-wildcards?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, if a keyframe animation is interrupting another animation, the transition will feel more natural.</p><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="wildcard-keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#wildcard-keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Wildcard keyframes</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">null</code> keyframe is called a <strong class="framer-text">wildcard keyframe</strong>. A wildcard keyframe simply takes the value before it (or the current value, if this is the first keyframe in the array).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Wildcard keyframes can be useful for holding a value mid-animation without having to repeat values.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> is the start of the animation, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> is the end of the animation. Therefore, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.2</code> places this keyframe somewhere towards the start of the animation.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Gesture animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React has animation props that can define how an element animates when it <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-gestures">recognises a gesture</a><!--/$-->.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Supported gestures are:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileTap</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileFocus</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileDrag</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileInView</code></p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">When a gesture starts, it animates to the values defined in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">while-</code>, and then when the gesture ends it animates back to the values in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">initial</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="variants" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#variants" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Variants</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> prop works well for single elements, but real interfaces often need coordinated animations across parent and child components. Variants solve this by defining named animation states that propagate through the component tree.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto;aspect-ratio:560 / 315;--aspect-ratio:560 / 315" data-width="fill"><article style="position:relative;width:100%;height:100%;border-radius:0px;transform:unset;cursor:pointer;overflow:hidden"><div style="position:absolute;top:0;left:0;height:100%;width:100%;background:center / cover url(https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/U4AIQ92NFkI/maxresdefault.webp) no-repeat"></div><button aria-label="Play" style="position:absolute;top:50%;left:50%;transform:translate(-50%, -50%);width:68px;height:48px;padding:0;border:none;background:transparent;cursor:pointer"><svg height="100%" version="1.1" viewBox="0 0 68 48" width="100%"><path d="M66.52,7.74c-0.78-2.93-2.49-5.41-5.42-6.19C55.79,.13,34,0,34,0S12.21,.13,6.9,1.55 C3.97,2.33,2.27,4.81,1.48,7.74C0.06,13.05,0,24,0,24s0.06,10.95,1.48,16.26c0.78,2.93,2.49,5.41,5.42,6.19 C12.21,47.87,34,48,34,48s21.79-0.13,27.1-1.55c2.93-0.78,4.64-3.26,5.42-6.19C67.94,34.95,68,24,68,24S67.94,13.05,66.52,7.74z" fill="#212121" fill-opacity="0.8" style="transition:fill .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1), fill-opacity .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1)"/><path d="M 45,24 27,14 27,34" fill="#fff"/></svg></button></article></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants are a set of named targets. allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="propagation" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#propagation" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Propagation</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants are useful for reusing and combining animation targets. But it becomes powerful for orchestrating animations throughout trees.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants will flow down through <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components. So in this example when the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ul</code> enters the viewport, all of its children with a "visible" variant will also animate in:</p><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, variants can be resolved differently for each animating element.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="animation-controls" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#animation-controls" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Animation controls</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Declarative animations via <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code> cover most UI interactions. For cases that need sequencing, timeline scrubbing, or triggering animations from events outside React's render cycle, the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate">useAnimate</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate"> hook</a><!--/$-->  provides imperative controls:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Animating any HTML/SVG element (not just <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex animation sequences.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Controlling animations with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">time</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">speed</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">play()</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pause()</code> and other playback controls.</p></li></ul><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This avoids React re-renders entirely. The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component updates the DOM text node directly, making it suitable for high-frequency value changes like counters or live data.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/html-content?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">It's also possible to <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="../">animate numbers</a><!--/$--> with a ticking counter effect using the <code class="framer-text class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/number-radix-slider?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="next" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#next" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Next</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In this guide we've covered the basic kinds of animations we can perform in Motion using its <strong class="framer-text">animation props</strong>. However, there's much more to discover.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Most of the examples on this page have used HTML elements, but Motion also has unique <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-svg-animation">SVG animation</a><!--/$--> features, like its simple line drawing API.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">We've also only covered time-based animations, but Motion also provides powerful <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-scroll-animations">scroll animation</a><!--/$--> features like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">useScroll</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileInView</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">It also provides a powerful <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-layout-animations">layout animation</a><!--/$--> engine, that can animate between any two layouts using performant transforms.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Finally, there's also a whole <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="https://motion.dev/examples?platform=react#fundamentals" rel="noopener">Fundamentals examples category</a><!--/$--> that covers all the basics of animating with Motion for React with live demos and copy-paste code.</p></div></div><div class="ssr-variant hidden-10pzhmj hidden-uq7ul"><div class="framer-a4hymo" data-framer-name="Content" style="transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="../">Motion for React</a><!--/$--> is a simple yet powerful animation library. Whether you're building hover effects, scroll-triggered animations, or complex animation sequences, this guide will provide an overview of all the ways you can animate in React with Motion.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="what-you-ll-learn" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#what-you-ll-learn" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">What first animation with the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">&lt;motion.div /&gt;</code> component.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Which values and elements you can animate.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to customise your animations with transition options.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to animate elements as they enter and exit the DOM.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">How to orchestrate animations with variants.</p></li></ul><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">These work identically to their HTML/SVG counterparts - same props, same behaviour - but with additional animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The most common animation prop is <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>. When values passed to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> change, the element will automatically animate to that value.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" 240));--border-left-width:1px;--border-right-width:1px;--border-style:solid;--border-top-width:1px;background-color:rgba(103, 219, 240, 0.2);border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px"><div class="framer-gv0uwh"><svg class="framer-QVbbS framer-bpvkia" role="presentation" style="--1m973uw:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));--js9iwy:2" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#746265957"/></svg></div><div class="framer-vb9j5w"><div class="framer-pwf8a9" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-200c00c6-377a-42d3-b88e-d67d5880022c, rgb(103, 219, 240));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1l6bcxe">For SVG components, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">y</code> <strong class="framer-text">attributes</strong> can be set using <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrX</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">attrY</code>. <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./react-svg-animation">Learn more about SVG animations in React</a><!--/$-->.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="supported-value-types" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#supported-value-types" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Supported value types</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion can animate any of the following value types:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">100</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Strings containing numbers: <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0vh"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"10px"</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors: All CSS color formats like hex, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rgba</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">hsla</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklch</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">oklab</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color-mix</code> etc.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex strings containing multiple numbers and/or colors (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">box-shadow</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">display: "none"/"block"</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">visibility: "hidden"/"visible"</code>.</p></li></ul><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="value-type-conversion" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#value-type-conversion" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Value type conversion</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In general, values can only be animated between two of the same type (i.e <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code> to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"100px"</code>).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Colors can be freely animated between hex, RGBA and HSLA types.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Additionally, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">width</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">height</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">top</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code> can animate between different value types.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transform-origin</code> has three shortcut values that can be set and animated individually:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="transitions" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#transitions" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Transitions</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, Motion will create appropriate transitions for snappy animations based on the type of value being animated.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For instance, physical properties like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code> are animated with spring physics, whereas values like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">color</code> are animated with duration-based easing curves.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">However, you can define your own animations via <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions">transition</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions"> prop</a><!--/$-->.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="exit-animations" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#exit-animations" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Exit animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React can animate elements as they're removed from the DOM.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In React, when a component is removed, it's usually removed instantly. Motion provides <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">the </a><!--/$--><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence">AnimatePresence</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animate-presence"> component</a><!--/$--> which keeps elements in the DOM while they perform an animation defined with the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> prop.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/exit-animation?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Keyframes</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">So far, we've set animation props like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">exit</code> to single values, like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">opacity: 0</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This is great when we want to animate from the current value to a new value. But sometimes we want to animate through a <strong class="framer-text">series of values</strong>. In animation terms, these are called <strong class="framer-text">keyframes</strong>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">All animation props can accept keyframe arrays:</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">When we animate to an array of values, the element will animate through each of these values in sequence.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">In the previous example, we explicitly set the initial value as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>. But we can also say "use the current value" by setting the first value to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">null</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://examples.motion.dev/react/keyframes-wildcards?utm_source=embed" style="width:100%;height:100%;border:0;position:absolute;top:0;left:0" allow="accelerometer; ambient-light-sensor; camera; encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, if a keyframe animation is interrupting another animation, the transition will feel more natural.</p><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="wildcard-keyframes" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#wildcard-keyframes" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Wildcard keyframes</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">null</code> keyframe is called a <strong class="framer-text">wildcard keyframe</strong>. A wildcard keyframe simply takes the value before it (or the current value, if this is the first keyframe in the array).</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Wildcard keyframes can be useful for holding a value mid-animation without having to repeat values.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div timing</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, each keyframe is spaced evenly throughout the animation. framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-transitions#times">times</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> is the start of the animation, and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> is the end of the animation. class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Gesture animations</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion for React has animation props that can define how an element animates when it <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-gestures">recognises a gesture</a><!--/$-->.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Supported gestures are:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileTap</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileFocus</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileDrag</code></p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileInView</code></p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">When a gesture starts, it animates to the values defined in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">while-</code>, and then when the gesture ends it animates back to the values in <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">initial</code> or <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#variants" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Variants</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> prop works well for single elements, but real interfaces often need coordinated animations across parent and child components. Variants solve this by defining named animation states that propagate through the component tree.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto;aspect-ratio:560 / 315;--aspect-ratio:560 / 315" data-width="fill"><article style="position:relative;width:100%;height:100%;border-radius:0px;transform:unset;cursor:pointer;overflow:hidden"><div style="position:absolute;top:0;left:0;height:100%;width:100%;background:center / cover url(https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/U4AIQ92NFkI/maxresdefault.webp) no-repeat"></div><button aria-label="Play" style="position:absolute;top:50%;left:50%;transform:translate(-50%, -50%);width:68px;height:48px;padding:0;border:none;background:transparent;cursor:pointer"><svg height="100%" version="1.1" viewBox="0 0 68 48" width="100%"><path d="M66.52,7.74c-0.78-2.93-2.49-5.41-5.42-6.19C55.79,.13,34,0,34,0S12.21,.13,6.9,1.55 C3.97,2.33,2.27,4.81,1.48,7.74C0.06,13.05,0,24,0,24s0.06,10.95,1.48,16.26c0.78,2.93,2.49,5.41,5.42,6.19 C12.21,47.87,34,48,34,48s21.79-0.13,27.1-1.55c2.93-0.78,4.64-3.26,5.42-6.19C67.94,34.95,68,24,68,24S67.94,13.05,66.52,7.74z" fill="#212121" fill-opacity="0.8" style="transition:fill .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1), fill-opacity .1s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1)"/><path d="M 45,24 27,14 27,34" fill="#fff"/></svg></button></article></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants are a set of named targets. useful for reusing and combining animation targets. But it becomes powerful for orchestrating animations throughout trees.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Variants will flow down through <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components. So in this example when the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ul</code> enters the viewport, all of its children with a "visible" variant will also animate in:</p><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This way, variants can be resolved differently for each animating element.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="animation-controls" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#animation-controls" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Animation controls</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Declarative animations via <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code> cover most UI interactions. For cases that need sequencing, timeline scrubbing, or triggering animations from events outside React's render cycle, the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate">useAnimate</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-animate"> hook</a><!--/$-->  provides imperative controls:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Animating any HTML/SVG element (not just <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Complex animation sequences.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Controlling animations with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">time</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">speed</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">play()</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pause()</code> and other playback controls.</p></li></ul><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">This avoids React re-renders entirely. The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component updates the DOM text node directly, making it suitable for high-frequency value changes like counters or live data.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div style="width:100%;padding-bottom:75%;position:relative"><iframe loading="lazy" encrypted-media; geolocation; gyroscope; hid; microphone; midi; payment; usb; vr; xr-spatial-tracking; clipboard-write;" sandbox="allow-forms allow-modals allow-popups allow-presentation allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-top-navigation allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation"></iframe></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">It's also possible to
